skill.name: codebase-analysis
skill.description: Analyze project structure, dependencies, architecture, and quality signals to help users understand an existing codebase.
skill.summary: Maps codebase structure, technology choices, architecture patterns, and quality risks.
skill.author: AI-Vibe-Prompts

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code formatting and ordinary words. The skill is not malicious, but it instructs agents to inspect environment file names and run project commands such as lint, coverage, audit, and outdated checks, which require user trust in the target repository.

medium_findings.title: Repo-Defined Command Execution Requires Trust
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends running project commands such as lint, test coverage, npm audit, pnpm audit, and npm outdated. These commands are normal for codebase analysis, but they can execute repository-defined scripts or contact package registries when used on untrusted projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and are legitimate for the skill goal. The risk depends on the repository being analyzed, so this is a publishable warning rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Environment File Discovery May Expose Secrets
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to look for .env files during project discovery. Listing or reading these files can expose secrets if the agent includes sensitive values in its analysis output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directly names .env patterns, but it does not instruct exfiltration or secret printing. The concern is contextual and should be handled with a warning.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution matches. The reviewed locations are Markdown inline code and fenced examples that describe files, search patterns, or report formats, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is plain Markdown in SKILL.md. There is no Ruby source file or executable backtick expression in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to ordinary prose, including the skill description and a UI library name. No evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain descriptive text and the phrase Ant Design. They do not define or invoke DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to a list of testing tools. No port scanning, host probing, or network discovery instruction was found at the cited line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line lists Vitest, Jest, Playwright, and Cypress as testing tools. This is not network reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Bash Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks agents to run project commands for linting, coverage, dependency audit, and dependency freshness. These commands should require user approval on untrusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly listed in the skill instructions. They are common development checks, but they can execute repository scripts or use network access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment File Enumeration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes .env files in discovery targets. This can be useful for project mapping, but agents should avoid reading or printing secret values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The location explicitly names .env patterns. The instruction is not malicious, but it creates a credible accidental disclosure risk.

content.user_title: Analyze Codebases for Architecture and Risk
content.value_statement: Large repositories are hard to understand before making changes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured discovery of structure, dependencies, architecture, and quality risks.
actual_capabilities: Maps top-level project directories and important configuration files.
Identifies frameworks, libraries, package managers, and build tools.
Reviews architecture patterns for components, APIs, state management, and file organization.
Checks code quality signals such as linting, testing, TypeScript settings, TODOs, and console logs.
Suggests a structured report with project overview, risk areas, and next steps.
Can recommend optional commands for linting, coverage, dependency audits, and outdated packages.
limitations: It is focused on JavaScript and TypeScript project conventions.
Command-based checks depend on tools installed in the target repository.
Large repositories may require targeted analysis by directory or feature.
It does not replace a dedicated security scanner or human architecture review.

use_cases.title: Onboard To An Existing Repository
use_cases.description: Create a concise map of project structure, framework choices, and major subsystems before starting work.

use_cases.title: Plan A Refactor
use_cases.description: Find architecture patterns, quality signals, and technical debt before proposing a refactoring plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare A Project Health Review
use_cases.description: Summarize dependencies, testing posture, TypeScript strictness, and risk areas for a team review.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Repository Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository at a high level. Summarize the project type, main directories, technology stack, and the first risks I should know.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Pattern Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the architecture of this codebase. Identify component patterns, API structure, state management, file organization, and places where conventions are inconsistent.

prompt_templates.title: Quality And Dependency Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess code quality and dependencies. Check linting, tests, TypeScript settings, package manager files, outdated dependency signals, and technical debt indicators.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Readiness Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a refactor readiness report for this repository. Include architecture constraints, risky areas, missing checks, recommended validation steps, and a prioritized action plan.

output_examples.input: Analyze a new React repository before adding a feature.
output_examples.output: A report with project overview, React stack details, key directories, state management notes, test coverage signals, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Review a TypeScript service for refactor planning.
output_examples.output: A structured assessment of build tools, TypeScript strictness, module boundaries, large files, TODO comments, dependency risks, and refactor priorities.

output_examples.input: Prepare an onboarding summary for a frontend monorepo.
output_examples.output: A concise map of apps, packages, shared components, scripts, test locations, framework conventions, and areas that need deeper review.

best_practices: Start with files and directories before reading implementation details.
Ask before running repository scripts or commands that use network access.
Avoid printing secrets from environment files or private configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not run package scripts from an untrusted repository without user approval.
Do not treat generated build output as primary source code.
Do not report raw secret values found in environment or configuration files.

faq.question: What kinds of projects does this skill handle best?
faq.answer: It is best suited for JavaScript and TypeScript repositories, especially React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, Angular, and Node.js projects.

faq.question: Can it analyze very large repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but large repositories should be analyzed in chunks by directory, package, or feature area.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It recommends commands such as lint, coverage, audit, and outdated checks. Users should approve these commands for untrusted repositories.

faq.question: Will it expose secrets from environment files?
faq.answer: It may identify environment file names, but agents should avoid reading or printing secret values.

faq.question: Is this a security scanning skill?
faq.answer: No. It can highlight dependency and quality risks, but it is not a replacement for dedicated security tools.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
